item.title: Chinese tech enthusiasts ‘raise lobsters’ in latest AI craze
item.description: OpenClaw takes China by storm as local governments encourage creation of AI agents for productivity gains

item.title: Li Ka-shing builds cash pile as family empire looks to its next deal
item.description: CK Hutchison has been selling assets across its portfolio but is unlikely to return capital to shareholders

item.title: UK price gap between first-time properties and bigger homes at record high
item.description: Buyers in the South East and London face the biggest challenge in trading up for more space

item.title: Drone maker backed by Erik Prince surges 500% in Wall Street debut
item.description: Swarmer jumped to a $380mn market cap after its IPO

item.title: BHP names Brandon Craig as its new chief executive
item.description: World’s largest mining company announces Mike Henry is stepping down from the top role

item.title: JPMorgan halts $5.3bn Qualtrics debt deal as AI fears chill demand
item.description: Bank along with Wall Street peers risk a high-profile ‘hung deal’ if they cannot revive transaction

item.title: FirstFT: Afghanistan says 400 killed in Pakistani strike on Kabul
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Australia raises interest rates, and Nvidia nears AI chip exports to China

item.title: Elon Musk’s lawyers sidestepped SEC team in Twitter case settlement talks
item.description: Lawyer for billionaire Tesla founder told judge they attempted to resolve matter without Wall Street watchdog’s counsel

item.title: Microsoft reshuffles AI team to catch up on Copilot and model building
item.description: Software giant changes AI scientist Mustafa Suleyman’s role to focus on model research

item.title: Nvidia nears AI chip exports to China after US approval delays
item.description: Chief executive Jensen Huang says company received flood of orders and American licences over past two weeks

item.title: US airlines say demand for travel is booming despite the war in Iran
item.description: Delta, United and American point to robust bookings among deep-pocketed travellers even as conflict boosts prices

item.title: Oil in Oman soars above $150 as buyers rush to replace Gulf barrels
item.description: Closure of Strait of Hormuz has caused growing ‘dislocation’ between global benchmarks and cost of physical supplies

item.title: Warner Bros Discovery’s mystery Singapore suitor
item.description: WTF WBD?

item.title: EU weighs curbs on national powers to block mergers
item.description: Several failed banking takeovers have prompted Brussels to consider changes to bloc’s rules

item.title: Arizona charges Kalshi with operating illegal gambling business
item.description: US state first to file criminal case alleging prediction platform offered wagers circumventing unregulated betting ban

item.title: US-backed group buys DR Congo copper-cobalt mine under minerals deal
item.description: Acquisition will create first major American-owned mine in the African country in more than a decade

item.title: Odey’s former compliance head accuses him of falsifying minutes of key meeting
item.description: Jack Satt also tells court that financier’s statements to FCA ‘did not align with my understanding’

item.title: EU moves to delay increase in bank capital requirements
item.description: Brussels plans to neutralise short-term impact of Fundamental Review of the Trading Book over competitiveness concerns

item.title: Senior NHS manager who advised Palantir to step down from role
item.description: Matthew Swindells was joint chair of four major London hospital trusts while also advising the US tech group

item.title: UK considers expanding vaccinations for meningitis B after fatal outbreak
item.description: Deaths of two young people spark debate over whether jab should be offered to teenagers

item.title: Drone-struck Russian LNG tanker threatens ‘ecological disaster’ in Mediterranean
item.description: Arctic Metagaz drifting between Malta and Italy’s Lampedusa after suspected Ukrainian attack earlier this month

item.title: Salesforce reckons software and debt do mix after all
item.description: The company is making use of one market trend to offset the unhelpful effects of another

item.title: 随着胜利资本提高报价，对雅努斯·亨德森的竞购战愈演愈烈
item.description: 这家总部位于得克萨斯州的资产管理公司提出的最新报价，使其与Trian和General Catalyst展开了竞争【原文：Texas-based asset manager’s new bid sets up contest with Trian and General Catalyst】

item.title: 这些AI龙虾是怎么回事？
item.description: OpenClaw 这款极具人气的代理工具，让人想起了“反技术浪潮”兴起之前的岁月【原文：OpenClaw’s phenomenally popular agent is a reminder of pre-techlash days】

item.title: 在欧盟施压下，乌克兰对俄罗斯输油管道的态度发生转变
item.description: 基辅批准对“友谊”输油管道进行维修，该管道曾用于向匈牙利和斯洛伐克输送俄罗斯石油【原文：Kyiv allows repair work to Druzhba pipeline that used to carry Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia】

item.title: 当亚马逊和京东展开激烈竞争时，受益的正是消费者
item.description: 这家中国企业面临的风险在于，随着其在国内和海外的扩张，资金消耗将日益加剧【原文：The risk for the Chinese contender is one of cash burn as it pursues expansion at home and overseas】

item.title: 是时候听到央行鹰派言论了
item.description: 货币政策制定者是能源冲击中的参与者，而非分析者或旁观者【原文：Monetary policymakers are players in the energy shock, not analysts or bystanders】

item.title: 加拿大亿万富翁斯蒂芬·史密斯收购《经济学人》27%的股份
item.description: 一位金融服务企业家在此次拍卖中胜出，此次拍卖吸引了众多富豪、家族办公室及媒体集团的关注【原文：Financial services entrepreneur wins auction that drew interest from wealthy individuals, family offices and media groups】

item.title: 海湾能源生产国面临的战争经济成本
item.description: 分析公司估计，累计损失已超过150亿美元【原文：Analytics companies have estimated the cumulative loss to be more than $15bn】

item.title: 宾利因利润下滑及缩减电动汽车计划而裁员
item.description: 美国政策调整、关税以及中国市场需求放缓，使豪华车制造商遭受重创【原文：Luxury-car makers have been hit hard by US policy changes, tariffs and slowing demand in China】
